title: Dog Uncontrollable Shaking and Paddling Legs: Emergency Triage Guide
meta_desc: Is your dog shaking and paddling their legs? This guide explains seizure activity, emergency urgency levels, and how to help your pet right now.
content_html:
What Is Happening to My Dog?

If your dog is lying on their side, shaking uncontrollably, and moving their legs in a rhythmic swimming or paddling motion, they are likely experiencing a generalized seizure. During these episodes, dogs typically lose consciousness and have no control over their movements. This behavior is a result of abnormal electrical activity in the brain and can be distressing to witness, but it is important to remain calm so you can assist your pet.

Is This a Veterinary Emergency?

Urgency Level: High. Yes, this is considered a medical emergency. While a single brief seizure may not be immediately life-threatening, it is a symptom of an underlying health issue such as epilepsy, toxin ingestion, or metabolic disease. If the paddling lasts longer than five minutes or if your dog has more than one seizure in a 24-hour period, their body temperature can rise to dangerous levels, leading to potential organ damage. You should contact an emergency veterinarian immediately.

How a Photo or Video Helps Triage

If it is safe to do so, capturing a short video of the shaking and paddling can be an invaluable tool for your veterinarian. Since many dogs stop seizing by the time they reach the clinic, a video allows the vet to see exactly what happened. This helps them distinguish between a true seizure and other conditions like syncopal (fainting) episodes or tremors, ensuring your dog receives the correct treatment as quickly as possible.

faqs.question: What should I do during the seizure?
faqs.answer: Move furniture away to prevent injury, keep the room dark and quiet, and never put your hands near the dog's mouth as they may accidentally bite.

faqs.question: Can dogs swallow their tongues during a seizure?
faqs.answer: No, dogs cannot swallow their tongues. You should never put objects or your fingers in their mouth while they are paddling or shaking.

faqs.question: What happens after the paddling stops?
faqs.answer: Your dog will enter the 'post-ictal' phase, where they may appear blind, confused, or wobbly. Keep them confined and safe until you can get them to a vet.
